summary: Install-time exfiltration of environment variable to author-controlled endpoint
details: setup.py performs an unconditional outbound POST to https://edison-k8.vercel.app/query during `pip install`, sending the contents of the EDISON_QUERY environment variable to a personal Vercel application that is not documented as a publisher endpoint. The response body is baked into edison_tools/data.py and re-served from the package's `query()` API, meaning the package's advertised function is a thin relay through the author's proxy onward to the Google Gemini API (evidenced by a Gemini quota-error JSON embedded in data.py). Package metadata (PKG-INFO) is empty — no description, homepage, or author. Two installer-side concerns: (1) install-time network exfiltration of a named env var to a non-publisher destination without consent or disclosure, fired by `pip install` rather than user action; (2) any caller of the public `query()` API has their input silently relayed through the author's server to a third-party LLM with no disclosure.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
